Article Title: ‘She made country proud’: Supreme Court allows Vinesh Phogat to compete in Asian Games trials
Article Text:
WFI issues a show-cause notice to wrestler Vinesh Phogat. (PTI photo)





NEW DELHI: The Supreme Court on Friday refused to interfere with the Delhi high court order allowing Olympian wrestler Vinesh Phogat to participate in the selection trials for the 2026 Asian Games , observing that the athlete had brought laurels to the country through her achievements in wrestling.“If anybody else, it would have been on a different footing. She’s made the country proud”, the apex court said.The top court’s order came on a plea filed by the Wrestling Federation of India (WFI) challenging the high court direction permitting Phogat to compete in the trials, scheduled for May 30 and 31. To ensure fairness, the court ordered video recording of the process and asked that independent observers nominated by the Sports Authority of India and the Indian Olympic Association oversee the trials.The HC had noted that Phogat had informed the International Testing Agency in December 2024 that she was stepping away from competition due to pregnancy, with plans to return later. After giving birth in July 2025, she resumed training, and the agency subsequently cleared her to compete from 2026.Also read: Vinesh Phogat’s comeback hits fresh roadblock as WFI moves Supreme Court against Delhi HC order The court observed that her absence during pregnancy prevented her from participating in the qualifying tournaments required under the WFI policy, leading to her exclusion from the trials.It prima facie held that the policy unfairly limited participation to medal winners from specific events, thereby excluding wrestlers such as Phogat.The court also criticised remarks made in the WFI’s show-cause notice relating to the Paris Olympics weigh-in controversy. Calling references to the episode as a 'national embarrassment' deplorable, the court said the comments appeared vindictive, especially since the Court of Arbitration for Sport had found no wrongdoing on Phogat’s part.The SC, however, flagged concerns with the manner in which the HC examined the matter but decided not to interfere with their order."These are not medical college admissions but national and international sports. It can't be like courts will interfere in this manner and disrupt the entire schedule", Justice PS Narasimha remarked during the hearing.Phogat has been asked to respond to the plea filed by WFI against the Delhi HC order permitting her to appear in the Asian Games selection trials. The matter will now be heard on June 1.
Article Summary: Supreme court refuses to interfere with delhi high court order allowing vinesh phogat to participate in the selection trials for the 2026 asian games. The top court’s order came on a plea filed by the wrestling federation of india.

Article Title: Shubman Gill on his haunches, Ravi Shastri asks 'Where's the fevicol?' after Sai Sudharsan's bizarre hit-wicket - WATCH
Article Text:
Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan (BCCI/IPL Photo)

NEW DELHI: Not once. Twice. Same dismissal. Same reaction. Gujarat Titans batter Sai Sudharsan suffered a bizarre dismissal during the IPL 2026 Qualifier 2 against Rajasthan Royals in Mullanpur on Friday, getting out hit-wicket in almost identical fashion to his dismissal in Qualifier 1 against Royal Challengers Bengaluru in Dharamsala.With the unusual dismissal, Sudharsan became the first batter in IPL history to be dismissed hit-wicket in back-to-back innings.GT skipper Shubman Gill , batting at the non-striker's end, was visibly stunned. The Gujarat Titans captain looked distraught and even collapsed to the ground in disbelief after watching his opening partner walk back in such unfortunate circumstances.After Rajasthan Royals posted a formidable 214/6 in 20 overs, powered by Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive 96 off 47 balls, Gujarat Titans got off to a flying start. Openers Gill and Sudharsan stitched together a century stand and appeared to have the chase firmly under control.Just when it looked like the duo would guide Gujarat Titans to victory, Sudharsan's innings came to an abrupt end. Attempting to put away a low full toss from Brijesh Sharma through the off side, Sudharsan lost grip of his bat after making contact. The bat flew backwards and crashed into the stumps, resulting in a hit-wicket dismissal.The crowd was left stunned, while Gill dropped to his haunches in frustration at the other end."Where is the fevicol? Where is the glue?" Ravi Shastri quipped on air.Chasing 215, Gill and Sudharsan made light work of the target, adding 167 runs in just 12.5 overs before the unfortunate dismissal ended Sudharsan's innings. Despite the setback, Gujarat Titans cruised to a seven-wicket victory over Rajasthan Royals.After the match, Gill was asked about Sudharsan's unusual dismissal."I don't think I've ever seen anything. No one's seen anything like that, two matches, back to back in a row. The way he got out. I saw some video on social media that taping his hand, I think I'm going to have to do that in the next match," Gill said.With the win, Gujarat Titans set up a title clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad on Sunday.
Article Summary: Sai sudharsan becomes the first batter in ipl history to be dismissed hit-wicket in back-to-back innings. Gujarat titans beat rajasthan royals by seven wickets to set up title clash with royal challengers bengaluru.

Article Title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks down in dugout after Rajasthan Royals' IPL 2026 exit - WATCH
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

NEW DELHI: As Gujarat Titans celebrated their place in the IPL 2026 final, a distraught Vaibhav Sooryavanshi sat quietly in the Rajasthan Royals dugout. The 15-year-old hid his face in his Orange Cap and wiped away tears, unable to mask the disappointment of seeing his team's campaign come to an end.It was a rare sight. Throughout the season, Sooryavanshi had played with freedom and a smile, fearlessly taking on some of the world's best bowlers and lighting up stadiums with his breathtaking strokeplay. But on Friday night in Mullanpur, the teenager was left heartbroken.He had done almost everything he could. A brilliant 96 off 47 balls powered Rajasthan Royals to a commanding 214/6. However, his valiant effort was not enough as Gujarat Titans secured a seven-wicket victory to book their place in the IPL final against defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru.Chasing 215, Gujarat Titans were led by captain Shubman Gill, whose unbeaten 104 helped his side reach the target with eight balls to spare in New Chandigarh. The knock overshadowed Sooryavanshi's second successive 90-plus score in the playoffs.Sooryavanshi had carried Rajasthan Royals' batting for much of the season, amassing 776 runs, smashing 72 sixes and rewriting record books along the way. Yet, in that moment, none of those achievements seemed to matter.Even as skipper Riyan Parag addressed the broadcasters during the post-match presentation, Sooryavanshi remained seated in the dugout, struggling to contain his emotions. A member of the support staff sat beside him, offering comfort to a youngster who had given everything for his team but fallen agonisingly short of the final hurdle."I can't really put it in words. There's one way where you just go and slog and then you get a lot of runs. But the way he's batted, it's not just slogging. It's not blind slogging. You can't do what you just did today if you were just going from ball one," RR captain Parag said after the match."He calculates, he assesses conditions. He does literally everything well. I still can't figure out how. But hopefully he keeps doing that. Hopefully he represents our country and brings more laurels to the country. And hopefully he keeps on playing for Rajasthan Royals and gets us to that second championship," Parag added.At just 15 years of age, Sooryavanshi showed that behind the fearless batting, towering sixes and record-breaking performances is a young cricketer who deeply cares about his team. Individual milestones and personal accolades could not ease the pain of elimination.His IPL 2026 journey may have ended in tears, but it also marked the arrival of one of the brightest young talents in the game. Rajasthan Royals' season is over, but for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, this feels like just the beginning.
Article Summary: Gujarat titans beat rajasthan royals by seven wickets to reach ipl 2026 final. Teenager vaibhav sooryavanshi scored 96 off 47 balls but it was not enough. Gujarat will face royal challengers bengaluru in the final.

Article Title: The Rs 7 crore bargain vs the Rs 25 crore gamble: How Jason Holder outshone IPL 2026's biggest auction buys
Article Text:
Jason Holder: The only clear winner

Cameron Green: The Rs 25 crore paradox

The smart middle-tier buys

Did the uncapped Indian gambles pay off?

Rs 39.4 crore for almost nothing

How we measured value: The methodology behind IPL 2026's top auction buys

During the IPL 2026 mini-auction in Abu Dhabi back in December 2025, every franchise walked out believing it had cracked the tournament before a ball had even been bowled. Cameron Green became the most expensive overseas player in IPL history after Kolkata Knight Riders splashed Rs 25.20 crore on him, banking on a genuine all-round cricketer who could anchor the batting and chip in with the ball. KKR later doubled down on the pace-and-firepower route by bringing in Matheesha Pathirana for Rs 18 crore.Then came Chennai Super Kings, shattering the uncapped-player ceiling not once, but twice, splashing Rs 14.20 crore each on Kartik Sharma and Prashant Veer.Sunrisers Hyderabad went after explosiveness by securing Liam Livingstone for Rs 13 crore, while other franchises continued chasing high-ceiling overseas stars, specialist roles and Impact Player flexibility.Five months later, the league table, and more importantly, the numbers are telling a very different story.KKR, despite the blockbuster spending, finished seventh. CSK ended eighth. Some of the biggest buys of the season barely played, some never settled, while a few simply could not justify the price tags attached to them.And while franchises were busy chasing auction-night headlines, the biggest takeaway from the auction may not be who performed best.It is how little value the group delivered collectively.According to a TOI Data Desk model that converts on-field output into rupee-equivalent value, franchises spent Rs 122.80 crore on the ten most expensive buys from the IPL 2026 mini-auction. The estimated value returned by those players? Rs 55.32 crore.In simple terms, the group delivered less than half of what franchises paid for them.Only two players generated a positive return: Jason Holder and Josh Inglis.For all the attention around the biggest bids, the smartest buy of the auction cost Gujarat Titans just Rs 7 crore. Holder, who initially spent time on the sidelines before forcing his way into GT's plans, ended the league stage with 13 wickets in eight matches at an economy of 7.34.Read those numbers in the context of the league average economy rate of 10.26, and Holder's season becomes even more impressive.In a year when bowlers spent most evenings trying to survive, Holder consistently controlled games. He gave GT flexibility. He bowled difficult overs. He chipped away at partnerships. He became the kind of player every franchise wants but rarely manages to find.Using a league-wide value model, Holder generated an estimated Rs 11.98 crore worth of on-field value for GT against a Rs 7 crore investment. That translated to a net gain of Rs 4.98 crore and a multiplier of 1.71x - comfortably the best return among the top 10 buys.The only other player to generate a positive return was Inglis. And that might frustrate Lucknow Super Giants supporters even more. Available only for the final stretch of the season, Inglis arrived late and immediately looked like the missing piece LSG had spent two months searching for.In just five matches, he produced 262 runs and generated Rs 9.01 crore worth of value from an Rs 8.60 crore investment. The margin was slim - a net gain of Rs 0.41 crore - but he still finished on the right side of the ledger.He reeled of scores of 13, 85, 36, 60 and 72 with high impact strike-rate. Had Inglis been available from the start, it is difficult not to wonder how different LSG's campaign might have looked.Perhaps the most fascinating player in the study is Green. Because depending on how you look at the numbers, he was both a success and a disappointment. Green produced the highest absolute output among all ten players.His 322 runs and seven wickets translated into an estimated Rs 12.71 crore worth of value - more than any other player in the group.The problem was the contract. KKR paid Rs 25.20 crore. That left Green with a net deficit of Rs 12.49 crore - the single largest negative number in the entire study.That does not mean Green failed. Far from it. In fact, he was the only genuine dual-discipline contributor among the top 10 buys. But mega-money signings are never judged in isolation. They are judged against expectation.Add to that the fact that Green was not cleared to bowl at the start of the tournament, impacting KKR's balance, and his season becomes one of the most intriguing ‘what if’ stories of IPL 2026.The players who came closest to justifying their price tags largely came from the Rs 7-9 crore bracket. Holder topped the list, Inglis followed. Ravi Bishnoi narrowly missed breaking even, delivering Rs 6.63 crore worth of value against a Rs 7.20 crore fee.Venkatesh Iyer was next.Bought by RCB for Rs 7 crore and used sparingly, Iyer still generated Rs 5.23 crore worth of value despite playing only four innings. His unbeaten 73 off 40 against Punjab Kings in Dharamsala became one of RCB's most important knocks of the season.Interestingly, the four best-performing contracts in the study all sat below the Rs 9 crore mark.When CSK spent Rs 14.20 crore each on Kartik Sharma and Prashant Veer, it felt like a statement. Secure young Indian players before prices rise even further. Unfortunately for CSK, the returns were mixed.Sharma, to an extent, justified much of the hype. His 295 runs translated into Rs 7.51 crore worth of value. But even then, the franchise still ended up with a net deficit of Rs 6.69 crore on the contract.The same cannot be said about Veer. Limited by injuries and opportunities, he generated just Rs 2.25 crore worth of value from a Rs 14.20 crore investment. Together, the duo cost CSK Rs 28.40 crore and returned less than Rs 10 crore worth of value.Then there are the contracts that became cautionary tales. Pathirana, Livingstone and Dar combined cost Rs 39.40 crore. However, their combined value return was effectively zero.Pathirana played one match before injury struck. Livingstone managed just two appearances. Auqib Nabi Dar never crossed the sample threshold required to generate measurable value. Availability issues, injuries and team selection decisions all played a role.And maybe that is the biggest takeaway from IPL 2026’s auction story. For all the noise around mega bids and headline buys, the smartest signings of the season did not necessarily come from the biggest price tags.Instead, they came from the middle tier: players bought with a clear purpose and used exactly that way.Holder became GT’s problem-solver with the ball. Bishnoi quietly delivered consistent returns throughout the season. Inglis and Iyer maximised their limited opportunities.On the other hand, several marquee spends - not helped by injuries, role mismatches, poor form, and team balance - essentially turned into non-factors within weeks.We analysed ball-by-ball data from all 70 IPL 2026 league-stage matches (through May 24), sourced from Cricsheet, for the ten most expensive buys at the December 2025 mini-auction. Mustafizur Rahman was excluded after being released by KKR, with Venkatesh Iyer replacing him as the next-highest mini-auction buy.For each player, we computed Value Points (VP) by combining batting and bowling contributions. To convert those Value Points into rupee-equivalent value, we calculated a league-wide exchange rate by dividing the total IPL salary commitment by the total Value Points generated by all 165 qualifying IPL 2026 players.Each player's ‘Rupee Value Delivered’ was calculated by multiplying their Value Points by this exchange rate. Net return represents Rupee Value Delivered minus auction price, while multiplier represents Value Delivered divided by Price Paid.
Article Summary: Cameron green became the most expensive overseas player in ipl history after kolkata knight riders splashed rs 25.20 crore on him. Kkr, despite the blockbuster spending, finished seventh. Some of the biggest buys of the season barely played, some never settled, while a few simply could not justify the price tags.

Article Title: IPL 2026 Orange Cap and Purple Cap after Qualifier 2: Sooryavanshi stays on top, Rabada surges ahead
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Kagiso Rabada

IPL 2026 Orange Cap after GT vs RR

Runs M Ave. SR 100s/50s Sooryavanshi 776 16 48.50 237.30 1/5 Gill 722 15 48.13 163.71 1/6 Sudharsan 710 16 47.33 159.55 1/8 Klaasen 624 15 48.00 160.00 0/6 Kishan 602 15 40.13 182.42 0/6

Rabada overtakes Bhuvneshwar in Purple Cap race

IPL 2026 Purple Cap after GT vs RR

Wickets M Ave. Econ. Rabada 28 16 20.78 9.43 Bhuvi 26 15 18.15 8.00 Archer 25 16 22.36 9.31 Kamboj 21 14 25.23 10.52 Malinga 20 15 25.35 9.33

NEW DELHI: The IPL 2026 Orange Cap and Purple Cap races witnessed another dramatic twist after Gujarat Titans defeated Rajasthan Royals in Qualifier 2 on Friday to set up a summit clash against Royal Challengers Bengaluru.Despite Rajasthan Royals crashing out of the tournament, teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continued to hold the Orange Cap with another breathtaking knock. The 15-year-old smashed 96 off just 47 balls in New Chandigarh, taking his tally to 776 runs from 16 matches this season.However, his heroic effort was not enough to take RR into the final.The biggest threat to Sooryavanshi’s Orange Cap now comes from Gujarat Titans’ opening duo of Shubman Gill and Sai Sudharsan, who once again starred in a crucial chase.Gill hammered a stunning century in Qualifier 2 to climb to second spot in the standings with 722 runs, while Sudharsan’s fluent half-century helped him move to third with 710 runs.With GT now advancing to the IPL 2026 final against RCB in Ahmedabad on Sunday, both Gill and Sudharsan will get one final opportunity to overtake Sooryavanshi in the race for the tournament’s highest run-scorer award.The top five run-getters list is currently dominated by batters from playoff teams.Sunrisers Hyderabad stars Heinrich Klaasen and Ishan Kishan occupy fourth and fifth spots respectively, with 624 and 602 runs.RCB batting icon Virat Kohli remains in contention as well. Kohli currently sits sixth with exactly 600 runs and could still make a late surge in the standings during the final.The Purple Cap standings also saw a major change after Gujarat Titans pacer Kagiso Rabada delivered another impactful spell in Qualifier 2.Rabada picked up 2/35 against Rajasthan Royals to jump to the top of the wicket-takers’ chart with 28 wickets from 16 matches.The South African speedster now leads RCB pacer Bhuvneshwar Kumar by two wickets heading into Sunday’s title clash. Bhuvneshwar is second in the standings with 26 wickets and will have a direct chance to challenge Rabada in the final.Rajasthan Royals fast bowler Jofra Archer finished his campaign in third place with 25 wickets from 16 games.Chennai Super Kings pacer Anshul Kamboj occupies fourth spot with 21 wickets, while Sunrisers Hyderabad’s Eshan Malinga rounds off the top five with 20 wickets from 15 matches.With one match left in the tournament, both the Orange Cap and Purple Cap races remain wide open heading into what promises to be a blockbuster IPL 2026 final.
Article Summary: Gujarat titans beat rajasthan royals in qualifier 2 to reach ipl 2026 final. Teen sensation vaibhav sooryavanshi continues to hold the orange cap. Kagiso rabada overtakes bhuvneshwar kumar in purple cap race.

Article Title: Anaheim Ducks quietly emerge amid stunning Toronto Maple Leafs superstar speculation across NHL offseason discussion
Article Text:
Auston Matthews' future with the Toronto Maple Leafs remains uncertain (Getty Images)

Anaheim Ducks linked with Auston Matthews in NHL trade speculation



A new wave of speculation about Auston Matthews being involved in a potential blockbuster trade has made the Toronto Maple Leafs' offseason a major talking point throughout the NHL . There’s no suggestion a move is imminent, but league insiders suggest the Anaheim Ducks could be a surprising team to watch if Toronto re-evaluates its long-term plans this summer.The speculation comes with the Maple Leafs facing another important offseason after another disappointing playoff exit. NHL trade rumours involving superstar players tend to gain traction during front-office turnover, and Toronto’s recent management changes have garnered attention across the league ahead of the new season.The Anaheim Ducks find themselves an unlikely player in the latest Auston Matthews NHL trade rumor . Sources inside the league say Anaheim has the right mix of young talent, cap flexibility and future assets to be in the mix if the Toronto Maple Leafs decided to ever move their franchise captain.The Ducks have only recently gotten back into the playoff picture after a few years of rebuilding. The roster added some young talent in Mason McTavish, Leo Carlsson and Cutter Gauthier, and there’s optimism around the organization’s future. Their progress has also led to talk in NHL news roundups about possible superstar additions.After repeated postseason failures, the Toronto Maple Leafs still need to build a clearer long-term vision.Auston Matthews is still the franchise centrepiece, but the mysteries surrounding roster construction and expectations for a championship continue to fuel NHL updates and speculation.Matthews also had injury problems in stretches of the 2025-26 NHL season, which resulted in less offensive production than in previous years. Still, he’s among hockey’s elite scorers and one of the NHL’s most valuable players.Any discussion about Auston Matthews moving immediately reshapes the NHL trade market because so few players combine his level of goal-scoring ability and franchise value.The fact that you are associated with Matthews shows how far the Anaheim Ducks have come since their rebuilding days.The Maple Leafs and Ducks should be involved in many of the NHL trade rumours, NHL updates and league-wide conversations throughout the NHL offseason.
Article Summary: Anaheim ducks linked with auston matthews in nhl trade speculation. The anaheim ducks have the right mix of young talent, cap flexibility and future assets to be in the mix if the toronto maple leafs decide to ever move their franchise captain. There’s no suggestion a move is imminent, but league insiders suggest the anaheim ducks could be a surprising team to watch.

Article Title: Iga Swiatek reveals surprising Rafael Nadal advice amid growing Roland Garros challenge from Marta Kostyuk
Article Text:
Iga Swiatek reveals surprising Rafael Nadal advice amid growing Roland Garros challenge from Marta Kostyuk (Image via Getty)



Iga Swiatek says Rafael Nadal’s advice and mindset continue to help her at Roland Garros

Iga Swiatek is moving forward at Roland Garros once again. The four-time French Open champion booked her place in the fourth round on Friday after beating fellow Polish player Magda Linette 6-4, 6-4 in Paris. The win also gave Swiatek a small measure of revenge after Linette defeated her earlier this season at the Miami Open. The victory was Swiatek’s third straight win at this year’s tournament and kept alive her hopes of adding another Roland Garros title to her collection. Next, she will face Marta Kostyuk, who arrived in the fourth round after defeating Viktorija Golubic in straight sets and extending her clay-court winning streak.After her latest win, however, Swiatek was not only talking about tennis. Instead, she shared how lessons from Rafael Nadal have helped her deal with difficult moments and stay focused during matches. Nadal, who won Roland Garros a record 14 times, spent time working with Swiatek earlier this year, and she says those conversations continue to help her on court.Speaking after her victory over Magda Linette, Swiatek explained that Nadal’s biggest influence was not just technical advice.“There was some technical tips, but the most you can take from Rafa actually is his approach to the game and also him showing it’s not always easy,” Swiatek said.“It’s like on TV when he lifts his trophy, there’s a lot behind the scenes. Even the best players in the world sometimes struggle. He used to get over it and work on this stuff and never give up.“Rafa is the perfect example of that and he could also share some stories with me, which is amazing because he’s really open to give advice and I really appreciate it.”Swiatek began working closely with former Nadal coach Francisco Roig at the start of the clay-court season. The partnership started after her coaching relationship with Wim Fissette came to an end. Roig was part of Nadal’s team during many of the Spaniard’s biggest successes and has also worked with Emma Raducanu and Giovanni Mpetshi Perricard.So far, Swiatek and Roig have worked together during tournaments in Stuttgart, Madrid and Rome. Although she has not won a title since the partnership began, her performances in Paris suggest she is finding confidence at the right time.Roland Garros has been one of the most successful tournaments of Swiatek’s career. She lifted the trophy in 2020 and then won three straight titles from 2022 through 2024. Over the last several years, very few players have managed to stop her in Paris.Now, with another fourth-round appearance secured, Swiatek remains one of the strongest contenders for the title. Her next challenge against Marta Kostyuk is expected to be much tougher, but the lessons she learned from Rafael Nadal may continue to help her as the tournament moves into its biggest stages.
Article Summary: Iga swiatek beat magda linette in straight sets to reach the french open fourth round. The four-time roland garros champion will face marta kostyuk in the next round. She says rafael nadal's advice and mindset continue to help her.

Article Title: NFL Trade Rumors: Dallas Cowboys linked to $76 million Arizona Cardinals star in potential blockbuster deal
Article Text:
The Dallas Cowboys have emerged as a potential trade destination for Arizona Cardinals pass rusher Josh Sweat, who signed a four-year, $76 million deal last offseason. Reports suggest Sweat is unhappy in Arizona and has missed OTAs, fueling trade speculation. Dallas, still looking to strengthen its pass rush after moving on from Micah Parsons, could view the 12-sack defender as a key addition alongside Rashan Gary ahead of the 2026 season.

Category Details Player Josh Sweat Current Team Arizona Cardinals Rumored Team Dallas Cowboys Contract Status Under contract Salary Cap Hit Part of 4-year, $76 million deal Trade Likelihood Moderate Latest Insider Update Reportedly absent from OTAs amid dissatisfaction Potential Return Draft capital and/or player package

Which teams are interested in Josh Sweat?

What insiders are saying about the trade rumors

Contract details and salary cap implications

How the trade could impact both teams

The Dallas Cowboys continue to be linked to strengthening their defensive line in the 2026 NFL season. One name that keeps coming up in terms of this search is Arizona Cardinals’ Josh Sweat.Recent reports have indicated that Sweat, who just signed a four-year, $76 million contract extension with Arizona, appears to be unhappy in the desert and has become a trade rumor target. And he could wind up in Dallas, among other places.Dallas appears to be one of the most logical destinations if Sweat becomes available. The Cowboys entered the offseason with major questions on defense after missing the playoffs in 2025. Their pass rush, once driven by Micah Parsons, lost its identity after the star defender was traded to the Green Bay Packers before last season.The front office has already tried to address the issue by acquiring Rashan Gary, but one addition may not be enough. Sweat would give Dallas another proven edge threat and allow the defense to attack opponents from both sides.Other teams could certainly inquire if Arizona opens trade discussions, but Dallas stands out because of both need and fit.A pairing of Gary and Sweat would instantly reshape the Cowboys' defensive front and reduce pressure on younger rotational players.The speculation gained momentum after Cardinals On SI's Donnie Druin identified Dallas as a sensible destination."The Cowboys are in search of more pass rush help after trading away Micah Parsons, and what better way to polish their room off with a former divisional rival that had a career best 12 sacks last season? Dallas is always looking for a move to get them closer to a Super Bowl, and this could be the final puzzle piece."That view reflects a growing belief around the league that Dallas is still searching for another impact defender before training camp.Sweat is only one season into his four-year, $76 million agreement with Arizona. That contract gives the Cardinals long-term control, but it also means any trade would involve significant financial considerations.There has been no sign of a no-trade clause, which could make negotiations easier if both sides decide a fresh start is needed. Arizona would likely demand serious compensation for Sweat’s production and contract status.Getting Sweat to Dallas would be a big boost for a defense that had trouble getting consistent pressure on quarterbacks in 2025. Last season, he had 12 sacks, 30 tackles and 13 tackles for loss, numbers that would immediately improve the Cowboys’ front seven.Arizona would get flexibility for future roster planning as well if it received valuable assets in return. It’s unclear how willing the Cardinals are to move on, but the link between Sweat and Dallas is one of the more intriguing defensive trade rumors out there in the NFL.
Article Summary: Dallas cowboys have emerged as a potential trade destination for arizona cardinals pass rusher josh sweat. Reports suggest sweat is unhappy in arizona and has missed otas, fueling trade speculation. Dallas could view sweat as a key addition alongside rashan gary ahead of the 2026 season.

Article Title: Coco Gauff left overwhelmed by Hailey Baptiste father’s ultimate "dad move" during Wang Xinyu clash at French Open 2026
Article Text:
Coco Gauff greets her mother Candi Gauff and father Corey Gauff (Getty Images)

Coco Gauff praises Hailey Baptiste’s fighting spirit after devastating injury



Defending champion Coco Gauff recently opened up after fellow American Hailey Baptiste suffered a serious injury during her second-round match at the French Open 2026. Baptiste was playing against Wang Xinyu when the unfortunate moment happened late in the first set. The 24-year-old lost balance while chasing a shot and landed awkwardly on her left leg, immediately collapsing to the clay in visible pain.The scary scene shocked players and fans at Roland Garros. Baptiste screamed while clutching her knee as her father, Qasim, rushed from the stands to help her. She eventually left the court in a wheelchair. Later, reports confirmed that Baptiste tore her ACL and also damaged the meniscus in her left knee. Speaking after her own win, Gauff admitted the emotional moment deeply affected her and reminded her of the close relationship she shares with her father.CoconGauff said it was heartbreaking to witness Hailey Baptiste’s injury, especially seeing her father run onto the court in panic. The American star explained that the scene reminded her of her own father and how he would react in a similar situation. She added that Baptiste has always treated people kindly no matter her ranking or career situation.Which is something she truly respects.The reigning French Open champion also praised Baptiste for overcoming challenges throughout her tennis journey. Gauff described her as someone who has spent much of her career proving doubters wrong. She has done that through hard work and determination.Gauff revealed that she contacted Baptiste shortly after the incident. She offered support and encouragement. While she admitted she did not know the full extent of the injury at the time, she expressed confidence that Baptiste’s resilience would help her recover strongly. Gauff added that injuries like this are painful to witness in any sport and wished her fellow American a successful recovery.Meanwhile, Gauff continues her title defense at Roland Garros. The American recently defeated Mayar Sherif in straight sets and is now set to face Anastasia Potapova in the next round. Ahead of the tournament, Gauff also referenced Rafael Nadal while discussing her mindset, saying she believes every tournament is a fresh start and prefers focusing on one match at a time instead of the pressure of defending her title.
Article Summary: Coco gauff has opened up after fellow american hailey baptiste suffered a serious injury during her second-round match at the french open 2026. Baptiste was playing against wang xinyu when the unfortunate moment happened late in the first set.

Where to buy Powerball tickets









The US Powerball draw held on 23 May 2026 has drawn significant attention nationwide, with players waiting eagerly for the outcome of an estimated $131 million jackpot. As the prize pool continues to grow, excitement has surged once again across the country, with Powerball remaining one of the most popular lottery games in the United States and known for its life-changing payouts.In each draw, participants select five numbers between 1 and 69, along with an additional Powerball number ranging from 1 to 26. Because every combination is selected through a fully random process, results remain completely unpredictable, which adds to the tension and anticipation surrounding each drawing.Beyond the main jackpot, Powerball also includes several lower-tier prize categories, giving players multiple ways to win even if they don’t match all six numbers. Still, the estimated $131 million top prize remains the primary draw, making the 23 May 2026 event one of the most closely followed lottery draws of the day.The results for Powerball lottery winning numbers for 23 May 2026 revealed...And the winning numbers are:4, 16, 41, 48, 66,The Powerball lottery is a number-based game where players aim to match randomly selected numbers in order to win prizes. It is operated across several US jurisdictions under the Multi-State Lottery Association (MUSL), giving it a wide reach and making it one of the most recognisable lottery games in the country, especially known for its massive jackpot prizes.At its simplest, Powerball is built around choosing numbers. Players pick five main numbers from a set ranging from 1 to 69, commonly referred to as the white balls. In addition to this, a separate Powerball number is chosen from a range of 1 to 26. The draw itself is conducted using either mechanical machines or certified random electronic systems, ensuring each result is entirely random and independent of previous draws. This unpredictability is a major reason the game continues to attract steady interest.The game runs on a regular schedule throughout the year, with draws held three times weekly—on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Saturdays at 10:59 PM Eastern Time.Select five numbers between 1 and 69. These are your primary game numbers.Pick one additional number between 1 and 26. This is the special Powerball number that can decide major winnings.If you don’t want to choose manually, select Quick Pick, and the system will randomly generate all six numbers for you.Purchase your Powerball ticket before the cut-off time for the next draw.Your ticket is automatically entered into the next scheduled live draw.Compare your numbers with the official winning numbers after the draw.For an extra $1, you can activate Power Play, which multiplies non-jackpot winnings by a randomly selected factor, increasing smaller prize payouts.Powerball tickets are widely available across the United States at everyday retail locations such as gas stations, grocery stores, convenience shops, and some airport outlets.Each ticket costs $2, while opting for Power Play increases the total cost to $3.
Article Summary: The results for powerball lottery winning numbers for 23 may 2026 revealed. The winning numbers are:4, 16, 41, 48, 66, and 26. Powerball is a number-based game where players aim to match randomly selected numbers in order to win prizes. It is operated across several us jurisdictions under the multi-state lottery association (musl)

Article Title: ICC unveils all-female officiating panel for Women’s T20 World Cup 2026
Article Text:
Representative image (Pic credit: ICC)

Claire Polosak set for sixth Women’s T20 World Cup



ICC

highlights commitment to representation

ICC Women’s T20 World Cup 2026 Match Officials:

ICC International Panel of Umpires: Lauren Agenbag, Kim Cotton, Anna Harris, Shathira Jakir Jesy, Kerrin Klaaste, Candace La Borde, Janani N, Nimali Perera, Claire Polosak, Vrinda Rathi, Suzanne Redfern, Eloise Sheridan, Jacquline Williams, Gayathri Venugopalan

ICC International Panel of Match Referees: Trudy Anderson, Shandré Fritz, GS Lakshmi, Michell Pereira

The International Cricket Council has announced an all-female panel of match officials for the upcoming Women’s T20 World Cup 2026, marking the third consecutive edition of the tournament to feature only women officials.The marquee tournament, scheduled to begin in England and Wales from June 12, will see 14 umpires and four match referees oversee 33 matches across the competition.Among the new inclusions are Candace La Borde, Gayathri Venugopalan, Kerrin Klaaste and Shathira Jakir Jesy, all of whom are set to make their Women’s T20 World Cup officiating debut this year.Veteran Australian umpire Claire Polosak will once again headline the officiating panel as she prepares for her sixth Women’s T20 World Cup appearance.Polosak is the most experienced official in the group, having already stood in 22 matches at the tournament across previous editions. Fellow umpires Jacquline Williams and Kim Cotton are also set to reach a major milestone, with both gearing up for their fifth World Cup assignment after officiating in 19 matches each.Nine officials from the 2024 edition have retained their places in the latest panel, highlighting continuity and consistency in officiating standards at the global event.The four-member referee panel includes returning officials Shandre Fritz, GS Lakshmi and Michell Pereira, while New Zealand’s Trudy Anderson joins the group for the 2026 edition.ICC CEO Sanjog Gupta said the panel reflected the governing body’s commitment towards increasing representation across all areas of the sport.“This panel of match officials for the ICC Women’s T20 World Cup 2026 reflects the ICC’s commitment to expanding the scope of representation to all facets of the sport,” Gupta said in a statement.“The ICC takes into consideration a comprehensive, holistic, and evidence-based account while assessing its sports officials and their decision-making performance.“We are delighted to see the progress made by these officials and the elevated standards of officiating they demonstrated during ICC CWC 2025.”Gupta further added that the tournament is expected to become “the biggest women’s sporting event in the world”, with the officiating panel playing a crucial role in maintaining elite standards throughout the competition.The 2026 Women’s T20 World Cup in England and Wales is expected to be one of the biggest editions in the tournament’s history, with growing global interest in women’s cricket continuing to push the sport into new territory.
Article Summary: The international cricket council has announced an all-female panel of match officials for the upcoming women’s t20 world cup 2026. The marquee tournament, scheduled to begin in england and wales from june 12, will see 14 umpires and four match referees oversee 33 matches.veteran australian umpire claire polosak will once again headline the officiating panel.

Article Title: ‘Future is here’: Mohammad Yousuf sees shades of a young, fearless Shahid Afridi in Vaibhav Sooryavanshi
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Shahid Afridi

Sooryavanshi’s brutal assault leaves SRH stunned

Also Read:

Cricket world in awe of teenage sensation

Poll Do you agree with comparison of Sooryavanshi to Shahid Afridi? Yes, both are explosive hitters No, they're different players

NEW DELHI: The cricket world is still struggling to come to terms with Vaibhav Sooryavanshi’s astonishing IPL 2026 Eliminator knock, and now former Pakistan batting great Mohammad Yousuf has joined the growing list of admirers with a comparison that instantly grabbed attention.Less than 24 hours after the 15-year-old Rajasthan Royals sensation demolished Sunrisers Hyderabad with a breathtaking 97 off just 29 balls, Yousuf declared that the teenager reminded him of a young Shahid Afridi.“15-year-old cricket sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi reminds me of @SAfridiofficial — young, fearless, explosive, full of energy. The future is here!” Yousuf posted on X.The comparison with Afridi — one of cricket’s most destructive hitters during his peak years — only added to the growing hype surrounding the teenage prodigy, whose fearless batting has become the biggest talking point of IPL 2026.Sooryavanshi produced one of the greatest playoff innings in IPL history during Rajasthan Royals’ 47-run victory over Hyderabad in the Eliminator at Mullanpur.The teenage opener smashed 12 sixes and five fours at an extraordinary strike rate of 334.48, tearing apart even Australia captain Pat Cummins with three consecutive sixes in one over.His stunning knock powered Rajasthan Royals to 243/8 before SRH were bowled out for 196.The youngster narrowly missed out on becoming the fastest centurion in IPL history, falling for 97 while attempting another six. However, he still shattered multiple records during the innings.Sooryavanshi now holds the record for the most runs by an uncapped batter in an IPL season with 680 runs, surpassing Yashasvi Jaiswal’s tally of 625 in 2023.He has also smashed 65 sixes this season, breaking Chris Gayle’s long-standing IPL record for most sixes in a single edition.Despite missing his century by just three runs, Sooryavanshi revealed that personal milestones were never on his mind during the innings.“I got to know after the game,” he said when asked about narrowly missing the fastest IPL hundred record. “My focus was on hitting a six. I will score centuries in future but the focus was on getting maximum runs for the team.”Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag admitted the team deliberately avoids overloading the youngster with advice.“We don’t have any conversations; just leave him alone. We give him a lot of batting practice and he does his thing,” Parag said.England pacer Jofra Archer , who played a crucial role with the ball in the Eliminator, summed up the growing fear among bowlers perfectly.“The more runs he scores, the more runs we have to defend. He can get 150,” Archer said.With Rajasthan Royals now set to face Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 2, the cricketing world waits to see whether the fearless teenager can produce another extraordinary performance on the biggest stage.
Article Summary: Former pakistan batting great mohammad yousuf compares vaibhav sooryavanshi to shahid afridi. 15-year-old smashed 12 sixes and five fours at a strike rate of 334.48 in ipl 2026 eliminator. Teenager narrowly missed out on fastest ipl century, falling for 97 off 29 balls.

NEW DELHI: Royal Challengers Bengaluru pacer Bhuvneshwar Kumar has climbed to the top of the IPL 2026 Purple Cap standings after RCB’s dominant win over Gujarat Titans in Qualifier 1 on Tuesday.The experienced fast bowler now sits level with Kagiso Rabada on 26 wickets, but edges ahead due to a superior economy rate and average. Bhuvneshwar has been one of RCB’s most reliable performers this season, striking every 13.61 balls while maintaining an impressive economy of 8.00.Rabada’s chances of reclaiming the Purple Cap ended with Gujarat Titans’ defeat, as GT were knocked out of direct qualification contention. He finishes the season with 26 wickets from 15 matches at an economy of 9.48.The race, however, is still not over. Jofra Archer remains the closest active challenger with 21 wickets for Rajasthan Royals heading into the Eliminator against Sunrisers Hyderabad. If RR qualify for the final and Archer produces a big performance across the remaining games, he could still threaten Bhuvneshwar’s lead.Further behind are Anshul Kamboj of Chennai Super Kings with 21 wickets and Rashid Khan with 19 wickets, though Rashid’s campaign could extend only if Gujarat somehow survive through Qualifier 2.For Bhuvneshwar, the equation is now simple heading into the IPL 2026 final. Another strong outing in the summit clash would not only strengthen RCB’s title hopes but could also help the veteran pacer finish as the season’s leading wicket-taker.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El rubius was eliminated from mrbeast's $1 million challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal challengers bengaluru (rcb) beat chennai super kings (csk) by 43 runs. Tim david, rajat patidar and devdutt padikkal all scored half-centuries. Rcb set a new record for highest total in ipl 2026 with 250/3.

Article Title: How Prince Harry turned one visit into the Invictus Games
Article Text:
Prince Harry's encounter with wounded soldiers at a sports competition sparked a global movement. Witnessing their resilience, he founded the Invictus Games, a multi-sport tournament celebrating veterans' strength. Image Credits: Wikimedia Commons

A truly moving experience with the indestructible human spirit

Prince Harry witnessed the resilience of wounded soldiers at a sports event. This inspired him to create the Invictus Games. The international competition celebrates veterans' strength and unity. It has become a global movement. Image Credits: Wikimedia Commons

The real medical science behind adaptive rehabilitation

The experience of seeing the scars and physical challenges that wounded soldiers face up close was an eye-opening one that completely changed the perception of Prince Harry . On a tour of an adaptive sports competition across the Atlantic Ocean, the determination of wounded soldiers made him realise something that would completely change his life. While everyone expected that charitable acts of royals were always about discreet check presentations and hospital visits, this one act became the foundation of a global movement.However, many years have passed since that crucial expedition, and today the legacy of the international competition has significantly influenced veteran rehabilitation efforts once again. Realising that the conventional healthcare offered in hospitals was merely a starting point for recovery, Prince Harry decided to develop a special platform that would be aimed at capturing the genuine spirit of military service. In particular, he found a solution to translate it into an athletic battle on an epic scale. Therefore, the project became an excellent example of how one can shift the world's attention not only from sympathy but also prove that unity is the key factor that allows overcoming all challenges faced.Indeed, it should be noted that the history of this exceptional international tournament has its true beginning rooted in a particularly powerful experience. As mentioned on The Royal Family website , the inspiration for the movement came unexpectedly during a trip to the Warrior Games in Colorado, United States, back in 2013.Witnessing the amazing unity, determination, and psychological recovery exhibited by wounded servicemen and women participating in the games, the prince realised that there was tremendous potential in sports competitions.Determined to replicate and expand this brilliant concept on a far grander, international scale, he instantly set a major plan in motion upon returning to the United Kingdom. The Games were developed through the Royal Foundation with support from the Ministry of Defence and partners. Choosing the meaningful Latin word for unconquered to serve as the defining title of the project, the founders aimed to celebrate the absolute resilience of veterans who flatly refused to be defined by their injuries.An extensive logistical undertaking was needed, as the idea developed into an all-out global event in the shortest time possible. At its inception in London in September 2014, the game involved more than 400 competitors from 13 countries at the Queen Elizabeth Olympic Park. By concentrating completely on the positive outcome for people after sustaining their injuries, the opening ceremony clearly showed that the platform was not simply an ordinary sports event; it was a vivid demonstration of the strength of the human spirit.The quick and sustainable success of this global game relies on much more than popularity, as there is a substantial scientific basis behind the initiative. Being engaged in sports brings about huge psychological improvement in those who have sustained their bodies. The results are impressive as competition helps overcome depression and regain individuality while creating a comfortable feeling of social belonging.By replacing medical isolation with intense daily training regimens and deep peer-to-peer teamwork, the international games provide injured service members with a vital new mission. As further explored in a systematic review investigating the Effect of adaptive sports on quality of life in individuals with disabilities who use wheelchairs, the physical routine of sport helps rebuild crucial psychological resilience. The researchers noted vast improvements in overall life satisfaction when individuals were given an empowering environment to test their limits alongside fellow wounded comrades.The total simplicity in making use of the language of sport made the growth of the initiative exponential, and left behind a heritage that still tours major cities across the world. The participants don’t see themselves as victims, but rather as world-class athletes with a high level of self-discipline. It is precisely this mindset shift that enabled the concept to evolve from an initial moment of inspiration to become a global organisation that transforms the way society sees its veterans.
Article Summary: Prince harry's encounter with wounded soldiers at a sports competition sparked a global movement. Witnessing their resilience, he founded the invictus games, a multi-sport tournament celebrating veterans' strength. At its inception in london in september 2014, the game involved more than 400 competitors from 13 countries.

Article Title: Francisco Cerundolo reveals wild reaction when brother Juan Manuel flipped Jannik Sinner match around
Article Text:
Francisco Cerundolo reveals wild reaction when brother Juan Manuel flipped Jannik Sinner match around (Image via Getty)

Francisco Cerundolo says Juan Manuel Cerundolo’s win over Jannik Sinner gave him extra motivation

Juan Manuel Cerundolo pulled off one of the biggest surprises of the 2026 French Open, and his brother Francisco Cerundolo was just as shocked as everyone else. While Juan Manuel was fighting back from two sets down against World No. 1 Jannik Sinner on Court Philippe-Chatrier, Francisco was busy playing his own second-round match against France’s Hugo Gaston on another court. At first, Francisco had no idea what was happening. He later told journalist Ale Klappenbach that he only started hearing updates during his own match. Those updates quickly turned into unbelievable news as Juan Manuel completed a stunning comeback to beat Sinner 3-6, 2-6, 7-5, 6-1, 6-1 in Paris. The win ended Sinner’s 30-match winning streak and sent the Argentine into the third round of Roland Garros.Speaking about the moment, Francisco admitted he was completely unaware of his brother’s fightback.“I didn’t know. I had no f***ing clue. I walked in here and it was set and I was down 4-1 or 3-1. At one point we’d said with my family that they were gonna split up and I looked up there and my family wasn’t there. That’s when I said: ‘hey, what happened?’.So I ask and they tell me I was down two sets to one. ‘Ah, alright, good, he’s fighting’, I thought.”The surprise did not stop there. As Francisco continued battling Gaston, more people around the court kept sharing updates about Juan Manuel’s comeback. Suddenly, what seemed impossible became real, and it pushed him to keep fighting in his own match.Francisco said hearing that his younger brother was close to beating the top-ranked player in the world gave him a huge boost. The Argentine revealed that every update increased his determination to stay in his own match and find a way to win.“Suddenly, I was in the fourth set, heading to the towel and people were telling me my brother was winning. Me suffering and then they’d say: ‘your brother won’. I thought: ‘f***, I’ve gotta win this’. It was all totally insane.”While Juan Manuel was completing the biggest win of his career, Francisco was also turning his match around. After dropping the opening set against Hugo Gaston, he bounced back strongly to win 2-6, 6-4, 6-2, 6-1 and book his place in the third round.The brothers have now set up exciting Round 3 matches at Roland Garros. Francisco, the tournament’s 25th seed, will face American player Zachary Svajda. Juan Manuel, meanwhile, will take on Spain’s Martin Landaluce as he looks to continue his dream run in Paris.
Article Summary: Juan manuel cerundolo beat world no. 1 jannik sinner in straight sets. His brother francisco was playing his own second-round match at roland garros. Francisco said he only started hearing updates during his own match. He said hearing his brother was close to winning gave him extra motivation.

Article Title: Shattering records and cultivating future stars
Article Text:
Mayura Shanbaug

Ashish Kumar Srivastava - MD and CEO of PNB MetLife

Sameer Bansal - Chief Distribution Officer of PNB MetLife

Chirag Shetty - Asian Games Gold Medalist & JBC 2023 mentor

Satwiksairaj Rankireddy - Asian Games Gold Medalist & JBC 2023 mentor

Disclaimer: Content Produced by PNB Metlife

The 7th PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship shattered records with over 8,200 young talent nationwide, earning global recognition and fostering a culture of resilience and determination in aspiring badminton starsIn a spectacular display of badminton prowess and youthful enthusiasm, the 7th edition of the PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship (JBC) etched its name in the annals of sporting history. Celebrating a staggering participation of 8257 young champions from across India, the championship not only broke its own record set in 2022 but also clinched the title of the ‘Most kids participating in a Badminton Championship in multiple cities’ as recognised by the World Record Certification Agency (WRCA).The grand finale unfolded at the Indira Gandhi Indoor Stadium in New Delhi, where the air was thick with competition, camaraderie, and the unmistakable passion for badminton. The event, graced by chief guests Chirag Shetty and Satwik Rankireddy, Asian Games Gold Medalists and JBC 2023 mentors, was a true celebration of the sport’s rising stars.The 10-city extravaganza, spanning the length and breadth of the country, showcased the unwavering commitment of PNB MetLife in nurturing young talents in the badminton arena. With participants exceeding 8200, it’s evident that the PNB MetLife JBC has become a beacon for aspiring shuttlers, highlighting the deep-seated passion for badminton among the diverse and talented youth of India.Beyond the glitz and glamour of the record-breaking numbers, the championship holds a special place in the hearts of those at the helm. Ashish Kumar Srivastava, MD and CEO of PNB MetLife, expressed pride in not just surpassing their own world record but also in contributing to India’s glory on the international badminton stage. The championship, he emphasized, is not merely a competition but a significant milestone in creating a pan-India badminton ecosystem.This edition of the JBC saw a strategic partnership with world champions Chirag Shetty and Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, who served as mentors for the young talents. Sameer Bansal, Chief Distribution Officer of PNB MetLife, acknowledged the pivotal role played by these champions in inspiring the next generation of badminton stars. The focus, according to Bansal, is on unleashing the latent potential of young shuttlers and preparing them for global excellence.The Delhi chapter of the championship witnessed some nail-biting action with over 1000 participants competing fiercely across various categories. From Ananya Talyan’s stellar performance in Girls Singles Under 9 to Divyansh Rawat’s triumph in Boys Singles Under 17, the young talents left no shuttle unturned in their quest for victory.The results from the Delhi leg not only showcased the raw talent but also highlighted the championship’s commitment to breaking barriers. The PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship is not just about records; it’s about fostering a culture of healthy living, determination, and resilience among the future champions of the sport.In the words of Satwiksairaj Rankireddy, the championship serves as an invaluable platform.---------------------------------------------------------------As we celebrate the successful culmination of the 7th edition of PNB MetLife Junior Badminton Championship , we take pride in surpassing our own world record for highest participation and for being recognized by World Record Certification Agency. Beyond the records, JBC represents a significant milestone in our mission to lay the groundwork for India’ s glory and success on the international badminton stage by creating a pan-India badminton ecosystem that nurtures champions at the grassroots levelThis year has been especially remarkable for PNB MetLife JBC as we joined hands with world champions Chirag Shetty and Satwiksairaj Rankireddy as JBC Mentors for the young talents associated with this tournament. They have played a pivotal role in inspiring young shuttlers to dream big and elevate their game and skills. We are eager for the next edition to unfold in our journey of unveiling the latent badminton potential across our nation and preparing them for global excellenceIt’s inspiring to witness the boundless talent and enthusiasm within our young athletes. My journey as JBC mentor has been incredibly fulfilling, as I got the opportunity to guide these budding champions and witness the journey of these young badminton stars. This championship is more than a competition; it is a platform that nurtures dreams, ignites aspirations, and, I believe, it is laying the foundation for future gold medalists in the makingTournaments like these serve as an invaluable platform for nurturing and harnessing the potential of our country’s young badminton stars. Witnessing the enthusiasm, passion, and skill displayed by these emerging shuttlers reinforces the belief that our country has an immense reservoir of talent. My association as a JBC mentor has been a source of immense pride, particularly given the role that the championship is playing in creating an incredible platform that is serving as a powerful catalyst, empowering young players and bolstering their confidence to become future champions
Article Summary: The 7th pnb metlife junior badminton championship shattered records with over 8,200 young talent nationwide. The grand finale unfolded at the indira gandhi indoor stadium in new delhi, where the air was thick with competition, camaraderie, and the unmistakable passion for badminton.
